title: That Asuka Shinji scene in Episode 9
display_title: That Asuka Shinji scene in Episode 9
wikitext:
[]: That Asuka/Shinji scene in Episode 9             Quote Post by TonyTH » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:06 pm Yo guys, I hate to just bring this up as I'm sure it's been discussed somewhere and you'll probably link me to another topic...but the search function isn't giving me any luck, and I see you guys do it enough and not seem to care sooo... I hear murmurs while lurking that Asuka was trying to led Shinji on or something. And when she went into the other room and he didn't follow THAT'S why she got really mad. Anyone know of said incident?

[]: Re: That Asuka/Shinji scene in Episode 9             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:09 pm TonyTH wrote: I hear murmurs while lurking that Asuka was trying to led Shinji on or something. And when she went into the other room and he didn't follow THAT'S why she got really mad. Anyone know of said incident?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:10 pm This should answer any possible question on the AxS pairing you may have.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat Feb 05, 2011 12:51 pm You know, here's something funny I find with Asuka (and I have no idea if teenage females act like this or it's just with her); she always tries to place hints that she wants to get closer with Shinji (as that page demonstrates), yet her intentions never come across with him. But here's the thing that interests me, and I find it intriguing that the page hasn't mentioned this yet: she should know this, and I think she did. She knows well that Shinji isn't really going to do anything in reaction, she knows he's not going to get the message as soon as possible and she knows darn well that he will never become the man she wants him to be, and we have proof that she reacts with this in frustration and anger within herself. In fact, I think she knew all of this even before the mind rape; the only thing Armisael did was to give her visual proof of what she feared, and the impact of that episode isn't about shedding some light on what was in her subconscious all this time, but rather deals with her admitting to confirm those fears for all the world she so happens to 'hate' to see. So to extend this thread, I pose a question for the forums in order to think about what I just pondered: Did she knew all of these events were in deaf's ears but decides to go with it anyways and then becomes frustrated that it doesn't go her way, or did she had hope he would get it, and falls into frustration because it didn't work, all before the mind rape? I mean, obviously there's something between them, but I think if shippers are indeed going to ship them, we must find out if her intentions are sincere or manipulative for her own purposes before episode 22 pretty much psychoanalyze our beloved character and gave reaffirmation of what we know...if that makes sense. Come on AxS shippers, here's your chance to lend your magic on me! :)

[]:             Quote Post by CommieGuy » Sat Feb 05, 2011 1:29 pm C.A.P., I think you meant Arael, no? About your question... Well, I guess Asuka had hope(maybe she was even certain of it) that she could make him do something, because she thought she was the best and could do anything. She took it as a challenge, and knowing her(a person who can't stand to "lose"), became frustrated when her attempts failed. I think I said some nonsense here... gotta rewatch the series...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 05, 2011 1:38 pm A lot of analysis of that scene appears in the thread titled Misato's Apartment Layout , as one would of course expect.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat Feb 05, 2011 1:43 pm CommieGuy wrote: C.A.P., I think you meant Arael, no?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Feb 05, 2011 2:43 pm C.A.P. wrote: You know, here's something funny I find with Asuka (and I have no ideaSo to extend this thread, I pose a question for the forums in order to think about what I just pondered: Did she knew all of these events were in deaf's ears but decides to go with it anyways and then becomes frustrated that it doesn't go her way, or did she had hope he would get it, and falls into frustration because it didn't work, all before the mind rape? I mean, obviously there's something between them, but I think if shippers are indeed going to ship them, we must find out if her intentions are sincere or manipulative for her own purposes before episode 22 pretty much psychoanalyze our beloved character and gave reaffirmation of what we know...if that makes sense. Come on AxS shippers, here's your chance to lend your magic on me! :)

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sat Feb 05, 2011 2:57 pm esselfortium wrote: The personality she presents to the world is an adorable jerkass, not the wicked witch of the west, and we're not shown much of anything but vulnerability, fear, and a desire to be wanted when we get to see beyond it. I don't think there's really evidence with which to assume her motives were anything but genuine: she thinks she knows a lot more about people and the world around her than she really does, and as such her attempts to attract Shinji's affection are earnest but absolutely flawed.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Sat Feb 05, 2011 4:13 pm C.A.P. wrote: Good point, but my question relates to the notion that she already knows the reaction coming from those earnest affections to Shinji, whenever or not they would or would not materialize and whenever or not she's aware of those reactions. I mean, like I said, if people are going to ship the two, they should at least figure out if the earnest feeling is indeed true or controlled on purpose.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Feb 05, 2011 6:39 pm C.A.P. wrote: She knows well that Shinji isn't really going to do anything in reaction, she knows he's not going to get the message as soon as possible and she knows darn well that he will never become the man she wants him to be... Did she knew all of these events were in deaf's ears but decides to go with it anyways and then becomes frustrated that it doesn't go her way, or did she had hope he would get it, and falls into frustration because it didn't work, all before the mind rape?

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Sat Feb 05, 2011 7:00 pm C.A.P. wrote: So to extend this thread, I pose a question for the forums in order to think about what I just pondered: Did she knew all of these events were in deaf's ears but decides to go with it anyways and then becomes frustrated that it doesn't go her way, or did she had hope he would get it, and falls into frustration because it didn't work?

[]:             Quote Post by FES » Fri Feb 11, 2011 12:11 am C.A.P. wrote: Good point, but my question relates to the notion that she already knows the reaction coming from those earnest affections to Shinji, whenever or not they would or would not materialize and whenever or not she's aware of those reactions.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Feb 11, 2011 12:17 am FES wrote: I don't believe Asuka's true intentions regarding her (possible) romantic relationship with Shinji in Rebuild were ever stated in the movie. She never outright says, "I'm giving up on Shinji" or anything even similar. In fact, she says nothing at all.

[]:             Quote Post by FES » Fri Feb 11, 2011 12:21 am Eric Blair wrote: That's a very good point, but I was referring to the original series, not the original movie animations.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Feb 11, 2011 5:55 pm Eric Blair wrote: I can't talk for the others, and certainly not for what Anno was trying to show in the series, but I always got the idea that Asuka was trying and she kept trying because deep inside of her, she was was doing it hoping/expecting Shinji would get it/act on it "the next time". As for frustration, it was simply realizing that for all the wishful thinking, Shinji is the most inept leading man; Asuka seems to have an Electra complex to offset Shinji's blatant Oedipus complex... it's improbable the wimpy, whiny crybaby who shares your apartment will stop being that and be your leading man; once Asuka realizes that, and realizes Shinji is closer to Rei, she decides to give up.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Feb 11, 2011 6:08 pm die-yng wrote: You mean she gives up trying to get a reaction out of him? Certainly not her feelings for him.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Feb 11, 2011 8:06 pm I understand why you think so. Still, I think her behaviour after Arael does not really reflect her feelings toward Shinji, in fact her depression is so strong, that there seem to be no emotions left but anger and misery. I'd say that her line:" if I can't have you all to myself, then I don't want ANYTHING from you ." Is an indication there are still feelings left, otherwise she wouldn't even bother to say this. If, of course she's the real Asuka and not just Shinji's inner Asuka Avatar. I just wish Anno had shown Asuka's experience during 3I, because without any insight at all, all that is left is to guess.

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Feb 11, 2011 8:23 pm die-yng wrote: I understand why you think so. Still, I think her behaviour after Arael does not really reflect her feelings toward Shinji, in fact her depression is so strong, that there seem to be no emotions left but anger and misery. I'd say that her line:" if I can't have you all to myself, then I don't want ANYTHING from you ." Is an indication there are still feelings left, otherwise she wouldn't even bother to say this. If, of course she's the real Asuka and not just Shinji's inner Asuka Avatar.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Fri Feb 11, 2011 8:48 pm Eric Blair wrote: Which is what makes writing about it easier if you look at it that way.
